01. Datasets for Tensorflow CNN

09 January 2021 - 2 mins read time
Tags: Tensorflow 102

Source: Coding Everyday, towards data science

Sample Image Datasets

  • MNIST: Handwriting image set (60000,28,28)
  • CIFAR10: 10 classified image set(50000,32,32)
import tensorflow as tf

(mnist_X, mnist_y), _ = tf.keras.datasets.mnist.load_data()
print(mnist_X.shape, mnist_y.shape)

(cifar_X, cifar_y), _ = tf.keras.datasets.cifar10.load_data()
print(cifar_X.shape, cifar_y.shape)

(60000, 28, 28) (60000,)
(50000, 32, 32, 3) (50000, 1)

Mnist Datasets Visualize

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
plt.imshow(mnist_X[0], cmap="gray")
print(mnist_y[0])

5

Cifar Datasets Visualize

plt.imshow(cifar_X[0]) # frog
print(cifar_y[0])

[6]

Dimension Check

import numpy as np
d1 = np.array([1,2,3,4,5])
print(d1.shape)

d2 = np.array([d1,d1,d1,d1])
print(d2.shape)

d3 = np.array([d2,d2,d2])
print(d3.shape)

d4 = np.array([d3,d3])
print(d4.shape)

(5,)
(4, 5)
(3, 4, 5)
(2, 3, 4, 5)

# Mnist
print(mnist_y.shape)
print(mnist_y[0:5])

# Cifar10
print(cifar_y.shape)
print(cifar_y[0:5])

(60000,)
[5 0 4 1 9]
(50000, 1)
[[6]
 [9]
 [9]
 [4]
 [1]]

x1 = np.array
